title: oxalis pulchella var. tomentosa
intro: Oxalis pulchella var. tomentosa, also known as the hairy wood sorrel, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its high content of oxalic acid and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Oxalis pulchella var. tomentosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in folk medicine to aid in the treatment of conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
